reviews.review_msg: We were supposed to have two classes per week with him. He divided our class in 4 groups, and each group had 15/20min class per week with him. However, he just sent us YouTube videos to watch before class and we only discussed our questions. In other words, he did not teach us anything, he only answered questions

By far the worst instructor I have ever had the displeasure of dealing with at this institution thus far throughout my entire undergraduate career. He does not care about your grade or if you even learn anything, just that he is right and you are wrong. Very inflexible and does not have any concern towards any of the students.

He is setting you up to do poorly. Lectures are extremely boring and he seems like he has little experience. Entire grade based mainly on midterm and final which had failing averages after he gave everyone 5 extra points. There are 3 essays and I don't know anyone who even received a passing grade on them. Not sure how were going to pass.
Erik makes tough exams. lectures and ppt aren't really helpful. Gotta study all by yourself.
test very long can't even finish class average is not even 50%

His lectures are read straight from his powerpoints, which I found to be unhelpful and is delivered in a monotone pitch. During class discussions he will put you down in front of the class which is unprofessional and shows a lack of constructive teaching ability. In reference to the very unstructured class project do not expect helpful feedback.
very smart guy, 5 random quiz per semester but it isn't that hard. Exam and Mid term are base on the lecture note.
Avoid him. random pop quiz + hard exam

He is a tough grader. However, if you are interested in the course and ready to learn, he is a great option. He is knowledgeable in experimental economics,  knows the material very well and can be inspirational in experimental design. Never expect to sleep the course over and simply get a pass. Overall, he is one of my favourite!

He gives simple analogies about concepts, and solves the math problems step by step without skipping anything. If you're bad at calculus, it doesn't matter who the professor is, you'll find 201 hard, but Erik teaches it really well. His quizzes are an easy 20% of the grade and his tests are really fair.
